title: Field-like events should not be virtual
description: Field-like events are events that do not have explicit add and remove accessors. The compiler generates a private delegate field to back the event, as well as generating the implicit add and remove accessors. When a virtual field-like event is overridden by another field-like event, the C# compiler generates a new private delegate field in the derived class, separate from the parent's field. This results in multiple and separate events being created, which is rarely what's actually intended.
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

logic.reasoning: Detects virtual field-like events (without explicit add/remove). Virtual field-like events create separate delegate instances in derived classes, which is rarely intended.

should_not_trigger: public virtual event EventHandler MyEvent { add { } remove { } }
